description: The study of Classics focuses on the languages, literature, history, culture, and legacy of Greco-Roman antiquity. An undergraduate concentration in Classics furnishes students with a broad liberal education, and provides specialized training for those students intending to enter graduate school. Students may choose to study Ancient Greek, Latin, Sanskrit, and/or Modern Greek, and to explore courses in literature, mythology, history, philosophy, and religion. Students may pursue the standard Classics concentration or one of several optional tracks: Greek, Latin, Greek and Latin, South Asian Classics, Sanskrit, Greek and Sanskrit, or Latin and Sanskrit.

groups.name: One course in Greek or Latin on the 1000-level or above
notes: Options include: GREK 1110B, GREK 1110S, GREK 1111B, GREK 1150, GREK 1810, LATN 1020D, LATN 1040B, LATN 1060G, LATN 1110F, LATN 1110H, LATN 1110P, LATN 1820, LATN 1930B, and others (with instructor permission: GREK 2020E, GREK 2110K, LATN 2080F, LATN 2090I)

At least 3 of these 5 must be offered through the Department of Classics
